data.title: Zhejiang Expressway to Refresh Board as Merger Triggers Shanghai Compliance Rules
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Zhejiang Expressway Co ( (HK:0576) ).

Zhejiang Expressway Co., Ltd., a PRC-incorporated and Hong Kong-listed transport infrastructure operator focused on expressway-related services, is advancing an absorption and merger of Oceanking Development via share swap alongside a specific A-share issuance. These strategic moves place the company under dual-market regulatory oversight, including the Shanghai Stock Exchange’s standardized operation guidelines.

The company’s board has disclosed that independent non-executive directors Mr. Pei Ker-Wei and Ms. Lee Wai Tsang, Rosa have offered to resign after their continuous service exceeded the six-year threshold set by Shanghai’s self-regulatory rules for listed firms. Their resignations will become effective upon shareholder approval of replacement independent directors, prompting a governance reshuffle linked to the merger process while Zhejiang Expressway maintains compliance with Hong Kong Listing Rules and seeks new independent board members to safeguard regulatory alignment and investor confidence.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0576) stock is a Buy
with a HK$7.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zhejiang Expressway Co stock,
see the HK:0576 Stock Forecast page.

More about Zhejiang Expressway Co

Zhejiang Expressway Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong, operating in the transport infrastructure sector with a focus on expressway-related services. The company is currently pursuing an absorption and merger of Oceanking Development through a share swap and an issuance of A shares under a specific mandate, bringing it under the compliance framework of both Hong Kong and Shanghai exchanges.

The board of Zhejiang Expressway announced that independent non-executive directors Mr. Pei Ker-Wei and Ms. Lee Wai Tsang, Rosa have proposed to resign due to exceeding the six-year service limit for independent directors under Shanghai Stock Exchange self-regulatory guidelines. Their resignations, which will take effect once replacement independent directors are approved by shareholders, trigger a board refresh tied to the ongoing merger transaction, while the company emphasizes continued compliance with Hong Kong Listing Rules and its efforts to promptly identify suitable new independent directors.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited has announced a further delay in sending a shareholder circular related to a Subscription Agreement and the proposed issue of consideration shares under a specific mandate. An update from Logan Property Holdings Co ( (HK:3380) ) is now available.

Logan Group Company Limited is advancing its comprehensive debt restructuring, which includes a court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement with scheme creditors under the laws of the Cayman Islands and Hong Kong. The company’s efforts target a broad realignment of its financial obligations to stabilize operations and preserve value for stakeholders in a challenging property market.

The company has convened a scheme meeting for creditors and launched a consent solicitation to change the governing law of its equity-linked cash-settled securities from English law to Hong Kong law, facilitating implementation of the updated restructuring plan. These coordinated legal and creditor engagement steps are intended to streamline execution of the holistic restructuring and signal continued progress toward a more sustainable capital structure.

More about Logan Property Holdings Co

Logan Group Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed property developer operating primarily in the Chinese real estate sector, with both equity and debt securities traded on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company focuses on residential and related property projects and has been pursuing a holistic restructuring to address its capital structure and obligations to various creditors and holders of equity-linked securities.

The latest update is out from China Huajun Group Limited ( (HK:0377) ).

China Huajun Group Limited has agreed to issue 12,000,000 new shares to at least six independent professional investors at HK$1.09 per share, a modest discount to the latest market price and a steeper discount to the recent five-day average. The new shares, issued under the company’s general mandate, will represent about 12.84% of the enlarged share capital, and are expected to raise net proceeds of roughly HK$13 million to repay debts and bolster working capital, although completion remains conditional and investors are urged to exercise caution.

The subscription price was set after arm’s-length negotiations, referencing par value, prevailing market levels, and the group’s substantial audited net liabilities at year-end 2025. By strengthening liquidity and reducing leverage without creating a new substantial shareholder, the move aims to stabilize the group’s balance sheet while limiting changes to control, a consideration that may weigh on existing shareholders given the scale of equity dilution involved.

More about China Huajun Group Limited

China Huajun Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 377. It operates in diversified businesses and raises capital on the Hong Kong market, with a shareholder base that includes professional investors but no single new substantial shareholder from this transaction.

data.title: ContiOcean Redirects HK Listing Proceeds Toward R&D Partnerships and Vessel Platform
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest announcement is out from Contiocean Environment Tech Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2613) ).

ContiOcean Environment Tech Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-based environmental technology company listed in Hong Kong via H shares, focusing on marine-related systems and equipment. The Group develops prototype products such as low-flashpoint fuel supply systems for ammonia, carbon capture solutions and waste heat recovery systems, supported by dedicated R&D staff and cooperative research with universities and institutions.

The company leverages an ocean-going vessel as a maritime R&D and mobile exhibition platform to test and showcase its equipment and pipeline products, aiming to bolster its position in the maritime environmental technology segment. Its market focus lies in ship-based environmental technologies that help the marine industry improve energy efficiency and reduce emissions.

ContiOcean Environment Tech Group has announced a change in the allocation of approximately RMB119 million in unutilised net proceeds from its Hong Kong global offering. The board has rebalanced planned spending among R&D projects, recruitment and cooperative development, while confirming that the acquisition of a controlling stake in a vessel-based R&D and exhibition platform remains on track, now expected to complete by end-July 2026.

The revised plan trims funding earmarked solely for hiring 13 new R&D staff, extends the recruitment timeline to end-2026, and redirects part of the proceeds to cooperative R&D with universities, enterprises and research institutions through 2027. These shifts suggest a more flexible capital deployment strategy that could enhance innovation capacity and partnerships, while slightly delaying some talent expansion, with potential implications for the pace of product development and stakeholder expectations on growth execution.

More about Contiocean Environment Tech Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Shouhui Group Limited has announced the passing of non-executive director Sirui Li on June 25, 2026, noting that he had served on the board since January 2024 and was recognized for his significant contributions, leadership and guidance to the group. COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co ( (HK:1138) ) has shared an update.

COSCO SHIPPING Energy Transportation Co., Ltd. declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.38 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, approved by shareholders on 26 June 2026. For Hong Kong H-shareholders, the dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars at HKD 0.43707 per share based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.15019, with an ex-dividend date of 20 July 2026 and payment on 25 August 2026.

The company detailed the timetable for entitlement, including book closure from 22 to 27 July 2026 and a record date of 27 July 2026, and outlined differentiated withholding tax treatments for various categories of investors. The announcement underscores the firm’s ongoing shareholder return policy and clarifies tax obligations for resident, non-resident, and Stock Connect investors, which is important for income planning and compliance among its diverse shareholder base.

The latest update is out from Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2442) ).

Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited has announced changes to its board of directors and committee composition, underscoring the importance of independent oversight in its corporate governance framework. The company continues to align its governance practices with Hong Kong listing rules by rotating independent directors through critical board committees.

Independent non-executive director Dr. Gong Zhaolong has resigned due to personal commitments, stepping down from the Audit, Nomination and Remuneration Committees. He is replaced by semiconductor industry veteran Dr. Gong Bin, who joins the board and the same three committees, bringing nearly two decades of sector experience and multiple policy and academic roles that may strengthen the Group’s strategic and compliance capabilities.

Dr. Gong Bin’s appointment is for an initial two-year term, subject to annual general meeting re-election and subsequent rotation at least once every three years under the company’s articles. His role, remunerated with an annual director’s fee of HK$180,000, is structured to preserve independence and is expected to support the company’s governance, risk oversight and long-term strategic positioning for shareholders and other stakeholders.

More about Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited

Easy Smart Group Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through a group structure referred to as the Group. The company is overseen by a Board that relies on independent non-executive directors to staff key governance committees, including the Audit, Nomination and Remuneration Committees, in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

The Group emphasizes compliance with the Rules Governing the Listing of Securities on The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited, particularly regarding director independence and board rotation. data.title: Pa Shun Faces Receivership Over Key Pharma Assets After Scheme Default
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Pa Shun International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0574) ).

Pa Shun International Holdings has defaulted on a HK$10 million yearly payment due under its court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement, triggering enforcement of a debenture secured over assets of its key pharmaceutical subsidiary. The scheme, which had tied creditor recoveries to both fixed annual payments and a share of audited net profits through 2028, is now in default after the company failed to meet the 2025 payment deadline.

Following the default, the scheme administrator appointed Ernst & Young professionals as joint receivers and managers over the secured assets held by Toyot Pa Shun, the holding company for the group’s PRC pharmaceutical operations. The company is seeking legal advice and negotiating with the scheme administrator and receivers, warning that any disposal of these assets could lead to deconsolidation of its PRC subsidiaries and leave the group without sufficient operations, with trading in its shares remaining suspended.

More about Pa Shun International Holdings Limited

Pa Shun International Holdings Limited is an investment holding company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong. Through its indirect wholly owned subsidiary Toyot Pa Shun Medicine Factory Company Limited, it controls two PRC units engaged in manufacturing and distributing pharmaceutical products, which currently contribute all of the group’s revenue.

The announcement confirms Chairman Zhao Jie and CEO Maeck Can Yue as members of the board committees, with varying responsibilities across audit, nomination and remuneration functions. This allocation of roles is likely to shape how strategic decisions, risk management and corporate governance are coordinated at the board level, affecting stakeholders’ perception of accountability and control.

More about Miji International Holdings Limited

AI Health Technology Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1715, operates in the health technology sector. The company is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a conventional corporate governance structure for a Hong Kong-listed entity.

Executive leadership is headed by Chairman Zhao Jie and Chief Executive Officer Maeck Can Yue, alongside executive director Wang Jian. Independent oversight is provided by non-executive directors Shen Shujing, Lin Dongming and Zhang Yuanjie, who play key roles in board committees that supervise audit, nomination and remuneration matters.

data.title: Pharmaron Restructures H Share Awards Amid FX Policy Changes
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3759) ) has shared an update.

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. has restructured its employee H share incentive arrangements to comply with changes in foreign exchange administrative policies affecting its share award schemes. The move reflects the company’s effort to maintain continuity of staff incentives while adapting to evolving regulatory requirements for Hong Kong-listed issuers.

The company’s management committee cancelled 5,396,470 outstanding H share awards previously granted to 546 employees under its First H Share Award and Trust Scheme. It then issued a replacement grant of the same number of H share awards to the same employees under the 2025 H Share Award and Trust Scheme on substantially identical terms, representing about 1.53% of issued H Shares and 0.29% of total shares.

The new grant, dated July 2, 2026, carries a nil grant price and was made when the H Shares closed at HK$18.85, preserving the economic value of the awards for participating employees. All replacement awards are conditional on the employees consenting to cancellation of the original grants, ensuring that the transition between schemes does not dilute their entitlements or disrupt the company’s incentive structure.

with a HK$28.10 price target.

see the HK:3759 Stock Forecast page.

More about Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. is a China-based joint stock company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the pharmaceutical and life sciences sector. The company provides research, development, and related services, and uses H share award and trust schemes to incentivize its employees and align their interests with long-term corporate performance and market growth.

Its shares are traded as H Shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and the company manages multiple share award schemes approved by shareholders to support retention of key staff. These equity-based incentives form part of Pharmaron’s broader strategy to compete in global drug development and contract research markets.

Pharmaron’s capital structure includes both H Shares and domestic Shares, with H Share schemes representing a small portion of its total issued capital. The company’s governance framework empowers its board and management committees to implement and adjust share award plans in response to regulatory and market developments.

Scholar Education Group ( (HK:1769) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Scholar Education Group has continued to deploy its share award scheme as a core tool in incentivizing staff and aligning employee interests with those of shareholders. Operating in the private education sector with a focus on tutoring and enrichment services in China, the Hong Kong-listed company uses market-based share purchases to build a pool of shares held in trust for selected participants under its established scheme.

In its latest move, the trustee bought 416,000 shares on the market on July 2, 2026, representing about 0.062% of shares in issue, at an average price of roughly HK$1.01 and bringing the total shares held in trust to 34,122,000, or 5.06% of the company. The board argues the current share price significantly undervalues the firm, viewing the purchase as an opportunity to prepare future awards while signalling confidence in the business outlook and a commitment to ongoing, discretionary equity-based rewards for key stakeholders.

The company’s stance suggests it will continue monitoring market conditions and its trading price to time further acquisitions for the scheme. This strategy underscores management’s belief in the intrinsic value of the business and may be seen by investors as a supportive measure for the stock, even as it primarily serves to strengthen long-term employee engagement and retention through structured vesting conditions.

More about Scholar Education Group

Scholar Education Group is a Hong Kong-listed education services provider incorporated in the Cayman Islands and trading under stock code 1769. The group operates in the private education sector, focusing on delivering academic and related learning services to students in mainland China and positioning itself as a long-term player in the region’s after-school tutoring and enrichment market.

The company’s governance structure includes a board chaired by executive director Chen Qiyuan, with chief executive officer Qi Mingzhi and other executive and independent non-executive directors overseeing strategy and operations. Its listing on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong provides access to capital markets and supports the use of equity-based incentives to attract and retain key talent in a competitive education landscape.

Sichuan Expressway Co ( (HK:0107) ) has issued an update.

Sichuan Expressway Company Limited has announced that its principal place of business in Hong Kong will move to Rooms 2201-03, 22nd Floor, World-Wide House, 19 Des Voeux Road Central, with effect from 1 July 2026. The company’s telephone, fax and website details will remain unchanged, indicating that the shift is focused on office location rather than broader operational restructuring.

In conjunction with the relocation, the company has appointed Li & Partners as its process agent in Hong Kong, authorising the firm to accept legal process and notices under Part 16 of the Companies Ordinance. This change is expected to streamline the company’s handling of regulatory and legal communications in Hong Kong, supporting continued compliance as a Hong Kong-listed PRC infrastructure operator and providing clarity for stakeholders engaging with the company in the territory.

More about Sichuan Expressway Co

Sichuan Expressway Company Limited is a PRC-incorporated joint stock company engaged in the operation and management of expressway infrastructure, with its shares listed in Hong Kong. The company focuses on providing toll road and related transport services, positioning itself as a key player in regional highway connectivity between Sichuan and broader markets, with Hong Kong serving as an important offshore corporate and capital markets base.

The board currently comprises executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a standard governance structure for a Hong Kong-listed mainland infrastructure operator. This diversified board composition supports compliance with Hong Kong listing requirements and oversight of the company’s expressway operations and related investments across its portfolio in the People’s Republic of China.

data.title: Shandong Boan Wins CDE Nod for Phase II Lung Cancer Trial of Novel Immunotherapy Combo
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Shandong Boan Biotechnology Company., Limited. Class H ( (HK:6955) ) has provided an announcement.

Shandong Boan Biotechnology Co., Ltd. has secured approval from China’s Center for Drug Evaluation to commence a Phase II clinical trial of its anti-CD25 antibody BA1106 in combination with its PD-1 inhibitor BA1104 as a treatment for non-small cell lung cancer. The regimen targets driver gene-negative NSCLC patients in first- and second-line settings, a large segment of the lung cancer population that faces limited options once resistance to current immune checkpoint inhibitors develops.

BA1106 is the first investigational non-IL-2–blocking anti-CD25 antibody in China to enter solid tumor trials, designed to selectively deplete regulatory T cells while preserving IL-2 signaling and expanding effector T cells to boost anti-tumor immunity. Earlier Phase I data showed promising efficacy signals in several solid tumors and a favorable safety profile, and the new Phase II study will further assess efficacy and safety in immunotherapy-naïve and previously treated NSCLC patients while exploring broader use in other common solid tumors such as gastric cancer, potentially strengthening the company’s oncology portfolio and its role in next-generation immunotherapy.

More about Shandong Boan Biotechnology Company., Limited. Class H

Shandong Boan Biotechnology Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on innovative oncology therapies, including monoclonal antibodies and immuno-oncology drugs. The company targets major solid tumors such as non-small cell lung cancer and gastric cancer, aiming to address unmet clinical needs in patients who lack effective treatment options or have progressed on existing immunotherapies.

Its pipeline includes proprietary antibody candidates and biosimilar immune checkpoint inhibitors designed to enhance anti-tumor immune responses. By combining novel mechanisms like non-IL-2–blocking anti-CD25 antibodies with PD-1 inhibitors, Shandong Boan Biotechnology seeks to strengthen its position in next-generation cancer immunotherapy and compete in China’s rapidly evolving biopharma market.
